subUnits.title: 11.1 Mode of Formation & Economic Uses of Rocks
notes: • Magma rises from mantle through crust
• Intrusive: trapped in crust → slow cooling → coarse grains
• Extrusive: erupts as lava → rapid cooling → fine grains/glassy
• Erosion transports sediments via water, wind, ice
• Cementation binds particles with minerals (calcite, silica, iron oxide)
• Existing rock subjected to high temperature (>200°C) and pressure
• Contact metamorphism (near magma) vs Regional metamorphism (mountain building)
• Granite, basalt: roads, buildings, bridges, foundations
• Weathered rocks: source of soil minerals
• Traditional tools, grinding stones, cultural artifacts
• Niger Delta: sedimentary rocks hosting oil/gas
• Quarrying causes deforestation, dust, noise, habitat loss
• Sustainable mining requires reclamation, dust control, water management
• Rocks naturally recycle over millions of years
diagramDescriptions: Flowchart showing step-by-step sedimentary rock formation: weathering → erosion → deposition → compaction → cementation
Industrial uses chart: rocks → processing → final products (cement, steel, glass, fertilizer)
Map of Nigeria showing major rock/mineral deposits: Jos, Benue, Niger Delta, Oban
Photographic comparison: raw rock → processed material → end product
subUnits.youtubeQuery: Rock formation processes economic uses rocks Nigeria geography site:youtube.com

quiz.q: Sedimentary rocks form through the process of:
quiz.explanation: Sediments are compacted by overlying weight and cemented by minerals to form solid rock.

quiz.q: Limestone is industrially important for producing:
quiz.explanation: Limestone is the primary raw material for cement and is used in fertilizer and steel production.

quiz.q: Metamorphism occurs when rocks are subjected to:
quiz.explanation: Metamorphism transforms existing rocks through heat and pressure without melting them.

quiz.q: Crushed limestone is used in agriculture to:
quiz.explanation: Limestone (calcium carbonate) raises soil pH, making acidic soils suitable for crop growth.

quiz.q: Which Nigerian region is known for marble deposits?
quiz.explanation: Oban Hills in Cross River State contains significant high-quality marble deposits used in construction.

quiz.q: The transformation of shale to slate involves:
quiz.explanation: Shale undergoes regional metamorphism, developing cleavage planes to become slate.

quiz.q: A major environmental impact of rock quarrying is:
quiz.explanation: Quarrying clears vegetation, destroys habitats, and generates dust and noise pollution.

quiz.q: Coal and petroleum originate primarily from:
quiz.explanation: Fossil fuels form from accumulated and transformed plant/animal remains in sedimentary basins over millions of years.

quiz.q: Granite is preferred for construction because it is:
quiz.explanation: Granite's interlocking crystalline structure makes it extremely hard, durable, and resistant to weathering.

quiz.q: Sustainable rock exploitation requires:
quiz.explanation: Responsible mining includes site rehabilitation, dust/water management, and adherence to environmental regulations.

subUnits.title: 11.2 Mountains: Types, Formation & Characteristics
notes: • Elevated landforms rising sharply above surrounding areas
• Typically >600m elevation with steep slopes and limited summit area
• Formed by compressional forces when tectonic plates collide
• Rock layers buckle into anticlines (upfolds) and synclines (downfolds)
• Examples: Himalayas (India-Eurasia), Alps (Africa-Europe), Atlas Mountains, Appalachian
• Characteristics: parallel ridges, high peaks, sedimentary/metamorphic rocks
• Formed by tensional forces pulling crust apart
• Crust fractures, blocks uplift (horst) or drop (graben)
• Examples: Ruwenzori (Uganda), Vosges (France), Black Forest (Germany), Sierra Nevada
• Characteristics: steep fault scarps, flat summits, rift valleys nearby
• Formed by accumulation of lava, ash, and volcanic debris
• Built around volcanic vents over time
• Examples: Mt. Cameroon, Mt. Fuji, Mt. Kilimanjaro, Mt. Vesuvius
• Characteristics: conical shape, crater at summit, igneous rocks, fertile soils
• Former plateaus or highlands eroded by rivers/glaciers
• Remaining resistant rock forms mountainous terrain
• Examples: Scottish Highlands, Jos Plateau margins, Nigerian Eastern Highlands
• High relief, steep slopes, varied climate zones
• Difficult terrain limits agriculture and settlement
diagramDescriptions: Cross-section diagrams showing fold, block, and volcanic mountain formation processes
World map highlighting major mountain ranges: Himalayas, Alps, Andes, Ruwenzori, Mt. Cameroon
Labeled mountain features: peak, slope, base, fault scarp, crater, rift valley
Comparison table: mountain types, formation forces, examples, characteristics
subUnits.youtubeQuery: Mountain types fold block volcanic formation geography WAEC site:youtube.com

quiz.q: Fold mountains are formed by:
quiz.explanation: Plate collision creates compressional stress that buckles rock layers into folds (anticlines/synclines).

quiz.q: The Ruwenzori Mountains are an example of:
quiz.explanation: Ruwenzori formed by faulting and uplift of crustal blocks (horst) in the East African Rift system.

quiz.q: Mountains formed by lava accumulation are called:
quiz.explanation: Volcanic mountains build up from successive eruptions of lava, ash, and pyroclastic material.

quiz.q: A major advantage of mountains is that they:
quiz.explanation: Mountains force moist air to rise, causing orographic rainfall and feeding major river systems.

quiz.q: Which is a disadvantage of mountainous terrain?
quiz.explanation: Steep slopes and rugged terrain make road/rail construction expensive and technically challenging.

quiz.q: The Himalayas were formed by collision of:
quiz.explanation: Indian plate pushing into Eurasian plate created massive compressional folding, forming the Himalayas.

quiz.q: A graben is formed when:
opts: A crustal block drops between two faults
quiz.explanation: Tensional stress creates parallel faults; the central block drops to form a rift valley/graben.

quiz.q: Volcanic mountains typically have:
quiz.explanation: Accumulated eruptions build steep, conical peaks with summit craters or calderas.

quiz.q: Jos Plateau in Nigeria is best classified as:
quiz.explanation: Jos Plateau is an uplifted basement complex dissected by erosion, forming relict mountainous terrain.

quiz.q: Mountains contribute to climate regulation by:
opts: Creating microclimates and influencing rainfall patterns
quiz.explanation: Mountains force air uplift (orographic lift), creating varied climate zones and precipitation patterns.
